rulings.Case Title: MARINA GALLI VS. PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Arbitration And Consolidate Separate Arbitration Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday December 18, 2018 line 1. Petition To Compel Arbitration And Consolidate Separate Arbitration Proceedings The Court is inclined to GRANT the motion. By no later than 9:00 a.m. on December 18, 2018 Pacific Union International is to provide a declaration responding to the following questions: (1) Between January 2016 and March 2017 did the broker use the same form of Listing Agreement as is at issue in this case for any other listings handled by the broker? Yes or No. (2) If yes, has the broker ever initialed the arbitration clause? Yes or No. (3) If yes, what percentage (approximately indicate if it is more or less than 50%) of agreements contain the initials; and based on what considerations the initials are included/not included? (4) If the broker never initials the arbitration clause, why is this clause included in the Listing Agreement provided to the sellers for execution? (5) Who signs the agreement first, the broker or the seller? (6) Can the seller execute the agreement via DocuSign without initialing the arbitration clause, i.e. does the technology allow the omission of the initials before submission of the document via DocuSign? Yes or No. (7) Was the Plaintiff notified that she could omit the initials from the Arbitration Clause? Yes or No. (8) If yes, when was the Plaintiff notified, by whom, and in what manner? (9) Was the Plaintiff notified (prior to her receiving the Listing Agreement for execution) that Broker did not intend to initial the Arbitration Clause? Yes or No. (10) If yes, when was the Plaintiff notified, by whom, and in what manner? (11) Does the broker contend that the seller is bound by the arbitration agreement, i.e. that the Broker could have compelled the Seller to arbitrate had the seller not paid the commission? (12) If no, when did the broker notify the Plaintiff that arbitration clause is not a part of the Listing Agreement? (13) Has the broker initiated an arbitration (or moved to compel arbitration) with any seller based on a Listing Agreement which did not include broker's initials at any time between January 2016 and present? Please include the questions in the declaration. If the response requested by the Court is "yes or no," the response should be so limited. Defendant may decline to respond any or all questions and should clearly state so after reciting the question(s). Responses requiring a narrative should not exceed a total of 2 pages. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RAMESH SHRESTHA VS. HOTEL SUNRISE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday December 18, 2018 line 2. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Costs Hearing Required to address timeliness of the memorandum of costs, why the memo of costs is not premature. The Court notes that there is no copy of judgment or dismissal provided. If the Court determines that the Memo of Costs is timely, the hearing on the merits shall be on January 10, 2019. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID FINOCCHIO VS. SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion By Defendants Aaron Vermut And Adriana Vermut For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying Action And Request For Attorney'S Fees And Costs [Ccp §§1281.2,1281.4,128.7]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday December 18, 2018 line 3. Motion By Defendants Aaron Vermut And Adriana Vermut For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying Action And Request For Attorney'S Fees And Costs [Ccp §§1281.2,1281.4,128.7] GRANTED all claims against the sellers shall proceed in Arbitration; action is otherwise stayed. Defendant's request for fees and costs is granted. = (501/REQ)
